plans.audience: Employees, support, customer success, and partner support
plans.launch_message: Users and support teams will have clearer guidance when a gym check in does not work on the first attempt.
plans.internal_readiness: Support needs the fallback steps, customer success needs the employer message, and partner support needs the partner handling note.
plans.baseline: 2 failed check ins in the sample event data
plans.target_signal: Failed check ins should decrease and support contacts related to gym visits should not increase.
plans.risk_to_watch: The issue may be caused by partner operations rather than app guidance alone.
plans.decision_after_measurement: Decide whether to keep the fallback flow, expand partner guidance, or investigate partner specific failures.

plans.audience: Employees, customer success, and employer administrators
plans.launch_message: Eligible employees will receive clearer reminders that explain how to activate and find a useful first activity.
plans.internal_readiness: Customer success needs employer messaging and support needs the expected activation questions.
plans.baseline: 60 percent in the sample OKR data
plans.target_signal: Activation should move toward 75 percent without increasing support contacts.
plans.risk_to_watch: Activation may be blocked by eligibility data or employer communication rather than reminder content.
plans.decision_after_measurement: Decide whether to continue the reminder kit, adjust messaging, or inspect eligibility data.

review_questions: Which release candidate has enough evidence to move forward?
Which internal teams need enablement before launch?
Which metric will prove whether the release helped?
